summary: Iteration 6 (3 new features on top of iteration 5): Logo Spin, Memory Highlights (POST /api/chat/summarise + MemorySummary UI), Palette Autopilot. Backend: 15/15 pytest pass. Frontend: all 3 features + regressions verified. (1) LOGO SPIN — 8 bh-mark instances on / (Nav + SplashLoader + XeniaOS Sidebar + chat avatars); bh-disc animation-name='bh-spin' with duration=7s (≤10s), bh-disc-inner 4.5s; XeniaOS sidebar container has 7 bh-marks inside. (2) MEMORY HIGHLIGHTS backend — POST /api/chat/summarise: empty messages → 422 with 'messages is required' detail; only-assistant messages → 422 'no user messages to summarise'; happy path with user+xenia mix returns 200 with non-empty summary containing NO exclamation marks. Live Claude call verified (~4s). (3) MEMORY HIGHLIGHTS frontend — on /, clicked nav-chat; memory-summarise button initially disabled (no user msgs), enabled after 1s once user msg present. Click generated summary via /api/chat/summarise (~4s), rendered inside data-testid=memory-summary-result, and memory-summary-copy / -share / -regen buttons visible. Copy → clipboard content verified via navigator.clipboard.readText() after granting clipboard permissions (114-char summary text). (4) PALETTE AUTOPILOT — Ctrl+K opened palette; verified all 5 patterns: 'draft outreach to Northlake' → 'Draft outreach to Northlake Robotics' + 'AUTOPILOT · EMAIL WRITER' + Enter → XENIA · EMAIL WRITER active; 'open pipeline' → 'Go to Pipeline' + AUTOPILOT · MODULE + Enter → PIPELINE; 'research Orbit' → 'Research Orbit & Co.' + AUTOPILOT · AI RESEARCH + Enter → AI RESEARCH; 'signals from Solstice' → 'Show signals near Solstice Energy' + AUTOPILOT · SIGNALS + Enter → BUYING SIGNALS. Plain 'northlake' → NO palette-autopilot node; palette-item-co-c-northlake still visible; Enter fires first filtered item and closes palette. (5) REGRESSIONS — /admin login with xenia-admin-2026 succeeds → data-testid=admin-export-csv visible; Chat streaming (/api/chat/stream) verified via multi-turn message flow; Request Access email input on / visible.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/chat/summarise — Uses a fresh session_id (`summary-<uuid>`) per call, correctly isolating the summarisation from the user's chat session. System prompt forbids '!' and this is validated at runtime (test_chat_summarise_success asserts '!' not in summary). Non-blocking: the 422 for empty messages is raised AFTER the pydantic model has already accepted an empty list — this is fine but a `min_length=1` Field constraint would fail-faster at validation time.

MemorySummary.jsx — Sends the ENTIRE messages array on every summarise call (including the initial seed 'Good evening, Sara. ...' xenia message). Since the backend filters user messages to check the 'user messages exist' guard and includes both roles in the transcript, this is correct behaviour. `hasUserMessage` gate is computed with `m.text.trim()`, preventing enabling on whitespace-only submissions. Good.

paletteAutopilot.js — Pattern order is: draft/email → signals → research/brief → call/meet → open/show/view/go-to. This ordering matters because 'open pipeline' could conceivably conflict with a company named 'Pipeline', but findModule() is tried first inside the 'open X' branch — safe. Non-blocking: the 5th pattern (`open X`) returns null if X isn't a module/company/contact, which correctly falls through to standard filtered results.

CommandPalette.jsx onKeyDown Enter handler runs `autopilot` first, then falls back to `items[0]`. This is the spec, but note: when autopilot is present, autopilot is NOT in `items[]` (it's rendered separately from the top pill), so its `rememberPaletteHit` will record an autopilot id like 'auto-draft-northlake' into paletteRecents. On a subsequent no-query open, that id won't match any static item (since interpretQuery() runs only when q is non-empty), so it'll silently drop. This is acceptable but could be avoided by skipping rememberPaletteHit for autopilot kind, or by handling autopilot ids in the recents render pass. Non-blocking.

BlackHoleMark.css — animation-duration 7s/4.5s produces a clearly visible spin (previously 14s/8s felt static). rotateX(72deg) is applied both on base transform AND on each keyframe — this is required because the animation shorthand overrides transform, so preserving the tilt inside the keyframes is correct. `will-change: transform` set on both discs to hint the compositor. Non-blocking: 8 spinning bh-marks on / means 16 continuously-animating layers; on a very underpowered device this could impact battery — consider `prefers-reduced-motion` (already handled: keyframes disabled) or gating spin=true prop by IntersectionObserver.
seed_data_creation: A few test_<uuid>@example.com AccessRequest rows + a couple of fresh 'summary-<uuid>' Claude sessions per pytest run — harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For clipboard tests, use `page.context.grant_permissions(['clipboard-read','clipboard-write'], origin='<full-origin>')` — NOT `context.grant_permissions` (name 'context' is not defined in the mcp_browser_automation harness). To detect the active OS module post-Enter, read `document.body.innerText` and search for `XENIA · <MODULE_UPPER>` — the topbar splits the string across multiple text nodes so TreeWalker/text-node search returns []. Also note: XeniaOS is embedded inline on / (no separate 'Launch XeniaOS' button); the OS starts on Dashboard and nav-chat opens AI Chat. Session ids: 'xenia.chat.session' (chat), backend fresh 'summary-<uuid>' for summariser (does not pollute chat session).
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional issues found. All 3 new features + 4 regression checks pass.
